cards.name: American Express Business Gold Card

welcome_offer.description: As High As 200,000 Membership Rewards points after spending $15,000 in eligible purchases on your new Card in your first 3 months of Membership. Welcome offers vary and you may not be eligible for an offer.

earning_rates.notes: First $150,000 in combined purchases from top 2 categories each calendar year, then 1X. Categories include: advertising (online, TV, radio at U.S. media providers), U.S. electronic goods/software/cloud providers, U.S. gas stations, U.S. restaurants (including takeout/delivery), transit (rideshare, trains, taxis, ferries, parking, buses, tolls, subways), and U.S. wireless phone service providers

earning_rates.category: Flights and prepaid hotels booked via Amex Travel

earning_rates.notes: After $150,000 annual cap on 4X categories

annual_credits.name: FedEx, Grubhub, Office Supply Stores Credit
annual_credits.description: Up to $20 in statement credits monthly after using the Business Gold Card for eligible U.S. purchases at FedEx, Grubhub, and Office Supply Stores. Enrollment required.

annual_credits.name: Walmart+ Membership Credit
annual_credits.description: Up to $12.95 statement credit each month after paying for a Walmart+ membership (subject to auto-renewal) with the Business Gold Card. Up to $155.40 annually.

other_benefits: Secondary rental car coverage (in U.S. and abroad when paying for rental with card and declining CDW)
Access to Amex Offers

sources.notes: NerdWallet 2026 review with annual fee $375, welcome offer up to 200K points, earning rates, and benefits
The Points Guy current offer page noting $375 annual fee and welcome offer details
Amex official card page with earning categories and statement credits

cards.notes: Annual fee increased to $375 on February 1, 2024. The 4X categories are dynamic based on your top 2 spending categories each billing cycle - this provides flexibility but requires tracking. The $240 in annual credits ($20/month on dining/deli/delivery, office supply, and FedEx) can largely offset the annual fee. 6 transferable airline partners and 3 hotel partners. The personal Gold Card ($325 annual fee) may be a better choice for many, but the Business Gold offers similar earning with business-friendly category structure.

cards.notes: DISCONTINTUED - American Express confirmed in September 2024 that the card is no longer accepting applications. Existing cardholders can continue using the card. The $95 annual fee, 3X at U.S. supermarkets (up to $6K/year), 2X at gas stations and Amex Travel, 0% intro APR for 12 months, and 50% bonus points for 30+ transactions per billing cycle remain for existing cardholders. No foreign transaction fees. No longer available to new applicants.

cards.notes: The Hilton Aspire is the premium Hilton co-branded card offering automatic Diamond status (top-tier). The $550 annual fee is offset by up to $809 in annual credits ($400 resort + $200 airline + $209 CLEAR+). No Priority Pass lounge access but offers premium on-property benefits. The free night rewards earned at $30K and $60K spend are valuable at high-end properties. Offer ends April 15, 2026.

cards.notes: The no-annual-fee Hilton card is a solid entry-level hotel credit card. The new limited-time welcome offer adds a Free Night Reward (up to 250,000 points) alongside the 70K bonus points. No Priority Pass or travel protections beyond basic purchase/extended warranty. Gold status at $20K annual spend is a reasonable goal for moderate spenders. Foreign transaction fee is $0, making it useful for international travel despite being a no-AF card.

cards.notes: The mid-tier Hilton Surpass card at $150 AF offers strong value with 12x at Hiltons and path to Diamond status. The $200 annual Hilton credits (4x $50 quarters) effectively offset most of the annual fee. The limited-time welcome offer adds a Free Night Reward on top of the 130K points — this is the first time a complimentary free night has been attached to this offer. Spending $15K earns a second free night. Diamond status at $40K spend unlocks space-available upgrades and executive lounge access. Offer ends April 15, 2026.

sources.notes: Detailed review: $250 AF, 175K bonus after $5K/6 months, Gold Elite, 15 elite nights, earning 6x/4x/2x, secondary rental insurance, trip delay after 12 hours
Eligibility chart confirms Bonvoy Bevy as currently available Amex Marriott card (the original $95 Marriott Bonvoy Amex is no longer open to new applicants)
Official Marriott page: 175K points offer, $250 AF, Gold Elite, 6x Marriott, 4x restaurants/supermarkets (cap $15K), 2x other, 15 elite nights

cards.notes: Note: The original Marriott Bonvoy American Express Card (formerly Starwood Preferred Guest Amex, $95 AF) is no longer open to new applicants. This profile covers the Marriott Bonvoy Bevy Amex ($250 AF, current offer 175K points after $5K/6mo, ends 5/13/26). The Bevy sits awkwardly at $250 AF with no complimentary annual free night — the similar annual-fee Marriott Boundless from Chase ($95 AF) includes an annual free night by default. The $15K spending requirement for a 50K-point free night is steep. Best value is from the automatic Gold Elite status and 15 elite night credits that stack with other Marriott cards for fast-tracking to Platinum (50 nights). No Priority Pass, no FHR/THC eligibility.

cards.notes: The $300 dining credit (up to $25/month) and $100 property credit at Ritz-Carlton/St. Regis help offset the $650 annual fee. Annual Free Night Award up to 85K points can be very valuable at high-end Marriott properties. 25 Elite Night Credits stack with actual stays. Points transfer to 39 airline partners at 3:1 ratio.

sources.notes: The Points Guy detailed review: $149 annual fee, $600 welcome bonus ($300 gift card + $300 statement credit), earning rates, and annual benefits
Chase official Disney Inspire page with earning rates, $100 park tickets credit, $10 monthly streaming credit, and 200 Disney Rewards Dollars annual bonus
CNBC on Disney Inspire launch: $600 welcome offer, $149 annual fee, $420 in annual perks

cards.notes: New premium Disney card launched February 2026 by Chase and Disney. Replaces/disrupts the previous Disney Premier Visa ($49 annual fee). The $600 welcome bonus ($300 Disney gift card upon approval + $300 statement credit after $1,000 spend) is the strongest Disney card bonus ever. The $149 annual fee is higher than the Disney Premier ($49) and Disney Visa ($0), but annual credits ($120 streaming + $100 park tickets = $220) largely offset it for frequent Disney travelers. The 3% at Disney locations and gas stations and 2% at grocery stores and restaurants provides solid everyday earning for Disney fans. Disney Rewards Dollars redeem 1:$1 on park tickets, resort stays, Disney store, movies at AMC, Disney Cruise Line, and airline purchases via Pay Yourself Back. Best for frequent Disney visitors who will maximize the annual credits.

cards.notes: Annual fee increased from $250 to $350 in March 2025. The $200 annual TravelBank credit significantly offsets the $350 annual fee for United flyers. The $150 Renowned Hotels credit and complimentary Premier Silver status add additional value. With 4X on United purchases and 2X on dining/streaming/other travel, frequent United travelers can earn substantial miles. The 10,000-mile award flight discount each year after the first anniversary is valuable for award bookings. PQP earning (1 per $20 spent, up to 18,000/year) helps toward Premier status. Note: The $0 annual fee United Gateway card may be better for those who don't fly United frequently.

other_benefits.name: Points Transfer
other_benefits.description: Transfer points to Citi's airline and hotel partners including Singapore Airlines, Air France/KLM, Emirates, JetBlue, and more at 1:1.

sources.notes: Official Citi page confirms 60K bonus, $95 AF, 10x on CitiTravel, 3x on travel/dining/supermarkets/gas, $100 annual hotel credit, no FTf.
Daily Drop review confirms current offer of 60K points, $100 annual hotel benefit, no FTf, travel protections.
TPG comparison of Strata cards confirms current 60K offer for Strata Premier.
CNBC review confirms $95 AF, 60K welcome bonus, 3x on dining/supermarkets/gas/travel.

cards.notes: Card was renamed from 'Citi Premier' to 'Citi Strata Premier'. No foreign transaction fees. $100 annual hotel credit offsets annual fee. Strong transfer partners. No FHR/THC eligibility. Confirmed travel protections include trip cancellation/interruption, trip delay, lost luggage, and MasterRental car coverage.

welcome_offer.description: 60,000 ThankYou® Points

annual_credits.name: $100 Annual Hotel Benefit
annual_credits.description: Once per calendar year, enjoy $100 off a single hotel stay of $500 or more (excluding taxes and fees) when booked through CitiTravel

cards.name: Costco Anywhere Visa® Card by Citi

welcome_offer.description: No traditional welcome bonus. Earning starts immediately upon card use.

earning_rates.notes: 5% cash back on gas at Costco and Costco Gas stations. On the first $7,000 spent per year (combined with other gas/EV); then 1% thereafter.
4% cash back on other eligible gas and EV charging (outside Costco). On the first $7,000 spent per year (combined with Costco gas); then 1% thereafter.
3% cash back on restaurants and eligible travel. Includes Costco Travel bookings.
2% cash back on all purchases from Costco and Costco.com.
1% cash back on all other purchases.

other_benefits.name: Worldwide Acceptance
other_benefits.description: Visa Signature benefits accepted at millions of locations worldwide.

other_benefits.name: Citi Entertainment
other_benefits.description: Access to Citi Entertainment for special event access.

other_benefits.name: Costco Travel
other_benefits.description: Exclusive travel bookings through Costco Travel.

sources.notes: Official Costco page confirms 5% at Costco gas, 4% on other gas, 3% on dining/travel, 2% at Costco, 1% default. No annual fee with paid Costco membership. No FTf.
Daily Drop review confirms earning rates and no annual fee.
CNBC confirms 5% on Costco gas, 4% other gas (combined $7K/year cap), no AF, no FTf.
Costco Travel page confirms no FTf, $0 AF with paid Costco membership, 3% on travel.

cards.notes: Requires a paid Costco membership ($60+ annually) to use the card. Cash back is paid out annually as a reward certificate redeemable only at Costco warehouses. The 5%/4% gas categories are capped at $7,000 combined spend per year, then 1%. No FHR/THC eligibility. No traditional sign-up bonus. No foreign transaction fees.

cards.name: Discover it® Cash Back

welcome_offer.description: Unlimited Cashback Match: Discover automatically matches all cash back earned at the end of your first year. No minimum spending, no maximum cap.

earning_rates.notes: 5% cash back on rotating bonus categories on up to $1,500 in combined quarterly purchases (requires activation each quarter). 2026 Q1: Grocery stores, wholesale clubs, select streaming services. Q2: Restaurants, home improvement stores.
Earn 1% cash back on all other purchases.

other_benefits.name: Cashback Match
other_benefits.description: Discover matches ALL cash back earned at the end of your first year — effectively doubling your earnings.

other_benefits.name: Free FICO Score
other_benefits.description: Free FICO credit score on your monthly statement.

other_benefits.name: No Annual Fee Forever
other_benefits.description: No annual fee; all rewards are pure upside.

sources.notes: WalletHub - Discover it Cash Back Editor's Review April 2026.
Bankrate - Guide to the 2026 Discover Cash Back Calendar, Dec 2025.

cards.notes: Discover is accepted by 99%+ of US merchants. International acceptance is lower than Visa/Mastercard. The rotating 5% categories require quarterly activation at Discover's website. The Unlimited Cashback Match is the card's standout feature — effectively a 100% bonus match on all first-year earnings.

cards.name: Wells Fargo Autograph® Card

welcome_offer.description: Earn 20,000 bonus points when you spend $1,000 on purchases in the first 3 months from account opening (worth $200 in travel).

earning_rates.notes: Earn 3x points on restaurant purchases.
Earn 3x points on travel purchases.
Earn 3x points at gas stations.
Earn 3x points on transit including trains, rideshare, and EV charging stations.
Earn 3x points on popular streaming services.
Earn 3x points on select phone plan purchases.
Earn 1 point per dollar on all other purchases.

other_benefits.name: Cell Phone Protection
other_benefits.description: Up to $600 protection against damage or theft when you pay your monthly cell phone bill with the Wells Fargo Autograph Card ($25 deductible).

other_benefits.name: Rental Car Coverage
other_benefits.description: Collision damage waiver coverage for rental cars.

other_benefits.name: Travel Emergency Services
other_benefits.description: Travel and emergency services including roadside dispatch.

other_benefits.name: My Wells Fargo Deals
other_benefits.description: Discounts in the form of statement credits at participating merchants.

other_benefits.name: Event Access
other_benefits.description: Access to exclusive concert and entertainment events via Autograph Card Exclusives.

other_benefits.name: 0% Intro APR
other_benefits.description: 0% intro APR for 12 months from account opening on purchases; ongoing APR 18.49%-28.49% Variable.

sources.notes: NerdWallet - Wells Fargo Autograph vs competitors review, Mar 2026.
The Points Guy - Wells Fargo Autograph named No-Annual-Fee Card of the Year 2026, Feb 2026.

cards.notes: The Wells Fargo Autograph was named 'No-Annual-Fee Card of the Year' by The Points Guy for 2026. Wells Fargo has added airline and hotel transfer partners including Virgin Red. Card has no foreign transaction fees, making it a strong travel card.

cards.name: Bilt Mastercard®

welcome_offer.description: 30,000 bonus points after spending $1,000 on purchases in the first 3 months from account opening.

earning_rates.notes: Earn 1x on rent payments up to $100,000/year via Bilt Alliance (no transaction fee).
Earn 2x points on dining purchases.
Earn 3x points on travel when paying rent through the Bilt app.
Earn 1 point per dollar on all other purchases.

other_benefits.name: Rent Payments
other_benefits.description: Earn points on rent payments without any transaction fee, unique among credit cards.

other_benefits.name: Points Transfer
other_benefits.description: Transfer Bilt Points to leading airline and hotel frequent travel programs including United, American Airlines, Hyatt, IHG, and more.

other_benefits.name: Cell Phone Protection
other_benefits.description: Cell phone protection against damage or theft when you pay your monthly bill with the card.

sources.notes: CNET review of Bilt Mastercard features and benefits.
The Points Guy - Bilt card 2.0 transition and eligibility details, Jan 2026.

cards.notes: NOTE: The legacy Bilt Mastercard (Wells Fargo) was discontinued on February 6, 2026 and replaced by Bilt Card 2.0 (Bilt Blue, Bilt Obsidian, Bilt Palladium). This file documents the legacy card. The Bilt Mastercard was the first no-annual-fee card to allow users to earn transferable points on rent payments without a transaction fee.
